english: (acc. to the Ssk.) the interior rounding of the abdomen. — 2. vb. 1. to mix together Sch.; to put together, collect, tsogs (tshogs) merit Lexx. 2. for btúm-pa (btum pa) Pth.: dgé-_o_dún (dge 'dun) dbu-zlúm (dbu zlum) zabs-rjén (zhabs rjen) clerics with their heads wrapt up and barefooted. 3. for _o_dúm-pu ('dum pu). — zlum-ril (zlum ril) globular Cs. — *zlúm-bu* (*zlum bu*) W. host, swarm, troop, crowd.
(or rtse? (rtse?)) a mole-like animal Ld. (whether the same as rdza-bra (rdza bra)?).

pf. zlogs (zlogs), fut. bzlog (bzlog), trs. to ldóg-pa (ldog pa), to cause to return: 1. to drive back, repulse, an army Dzl.; to dispel, expel, evil spirits Dom.; to send back. — 2. in a gen. sense: to send, dispatch, people to fetch something Dzl. frq. — 3. to turn off, divert, bsám-pa-las (bsam pa las) from an intention Dzl.; with blo (blo) to divert the mind from, to dismiss a thought, to give up, to banish from one's thoughts Thgy., ynyén-gyi (gnyen gyi) yduń-séms (gdung sems) zlog (zlog) dka (dka) it is hard to give up the love of kindred altogether Mil.; dei (de'i) tugs (thugs) slar (slar) zlóg-tu (zlog tu) ysol (gsol) we beg you to dismiss the thought of it Dzl.; to dissuade from Tar. 40, 5; to avert, injury, evil consequences, frq.; to prevent, nad-sél (nad sel) the healing of a disease S.g. — zlog-tábs (zlog thabs) antidote Ma. — 4. to subvert, overthrow (?). 5. mii (mi'i) ńo (ngo) to resist, to be unyielding, uncompliant Dzl.
Stg. a dance, zlós-gar byéd-pa (zlos gar byed pa) to dance, slób-pa (slob pa) to teach or learn dancing; zlós-gar-mḱan (zlos gar mkhan) a dancer.

I. 1. planet, yza (gza') bdun (bdun) the well-known seven heavenly bodies called in ancient times planets, viz. Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn; sometimes the ascending knot (Ssk. राहु) is added to the number, sometimes also the descending knot (Ssk. केतु), and then there are yza (gza') brgyad (brgyad) or yza (gza') dgu (dgu), eight or nine planets. The former seven denote also the days of the week: yza-nyi-ma (gza' nyi ma) Sunday. yza-zlá-ba (gza' zla ba) Monday, yza-mig-dmár (gza' mig dmar) Tuesday, yza-lhág-ma (gza' lhag ma) Wednesday, yza-púr-bu (gza' phur bu) Thursday, yza-pa (gza' pa) (or -wa (-wa))-sáńs (-sangs) Friday, yza-spén-pa (gza' spen pa) Saturday, and the signs for them in the calendar are ཉ, མ, ད, ལྷ, ཕུ, ས, སྤ; yzai (gza'i) ynód-pa (gnod pa) hurtful influence of the planets. — 2. yza-čén-po (gza' chen po), and often yza (gza') alone, = rá-hu (ra hu), hence nyi-zla-yzas-_o_dzín (nyi zla gzas 'dzin) or yzas-bzuń (gzas bzung) eclipse of the sun or moon, v. sgra-yčań (sgra gcan); acc. to Pth. every uncommon or alarming sidereal phenomenon seems to be personified as yza (gza'). — 3. symb. num.: 9. — 4. vulgo: rainbow. — yza-skár (gza' skar), 1. planets and fixed stars, nyi-zla-yza-skár (nyi zla gza' skar) the sun, moon, planets, and stars. — 2. constellation, yza-skar-ńán (gza' skar ngan) an adverse configuration S.g. — yza-kyím (gza' khyim) Cs. 'the place', more corr. 'the house' of a planet, the constellation in which the planet stands. — yza-nád (gza' nad) Cs. and Schr.: apoplexy; in W. it seems to be used only for epilepsy; yza (gza') póg-pa (phog pa) id.; yzá-pog-mḱan (gza' phog mkhan), yzá-brgyab-pa (gza' brgyab pa) epileptic. — bái-yza (ba'i gza'), sróg-yza (srog gza'), yšéd-yza (gshed gza'), má-yza (ma gza') grógs-yza (grogs gza'), bú-yza (bu gza'), dgrá-yza (dgra gza'), klúń-si-dar-yza (klung si dar gza') Wdk. and several more, are astrological terms, not to be clearly defined. — II. sometimes for bza (bza'), q.v. — III. W. rubble-stones, bowlders, detritus, yza-róń (gza' rong) ravine filled with detritus; a better spelling seems to be rdza (rdza).
1. v. yzábs-pa (gzabs pa). — 2. to magnify, multiply Sch.

1. v. bzan (bzan) and yzan-pa (gzan pa); yčań-yzan (gcan gzan), q. v. — 2. esp. W., commonly yzan-gós (gzan gos) plaid, = bla-gós (bla gos) v. bla (bla). yzan-stán (gzan stan) Zam. id.? rńul-yzán (rngul gzan) napkin, nif. Lex.

1. to eat, devour Cs. — 2. to gnaw, mostly fig.: tsér-ma (tsher ma) zabs-la (zhabs la) yzan (gzan) the thorn hurts, annoys, the foot Mil.; of clothes: to wear out C.; adj. yzán-pa (gzan pa) and yzán-po (gzan po) worn-out, threadbare; séms-la (sems la) yzan (gzan) it gnaws at the heart Mil., sróg-la (srog la) it preys upon life Mil., *ná-wa-la* (*na wa la*) C. it deafens the ears, = sún-byin-pa (sun byin pa); yzán-du (gzan du) skyúr-ba (skyur ba) (lit. to give to devour, e.g. a body to demons), to scorn, slight, despise Mil.; to throw away, squander, waste, lavish, gen. in the forms (čud (chud))-yzón-pa (gzon pa), ; són-pa (son pa), v. čud (chud).
